qas.q: What is the definition of JSIG?
qas.a: Joint SAP implementation Guide

qas.q: What is the NIST Special Publications 800-53?
qas.a: Supplemental guidance to JSIG

qas.q: What is the NIST Special Publications 800-53-A?
qas.a: Guide for assessing security controls

qas.q: What is CNSSI 1253?
qas.a: Security Categorization and Control Selection for National Security Systems

qas.q: What is the definition of RMF?
qas.a: Risk Management Framework

qas.q: JSIG term for Certification and Accreditation?
qas.a: RMF Assessment and Authorization

qas.q: JSIG term for Accreditation?
qas.a: Authorization

qas.q: JSIG term for Requirements?
qas.a: Controls

qas.q: What are the three JSIG levels of Accessibility (Protection Levels)?
qas.a:
Baseline
Baseline +Accessibility Overlay
Baseline + CDS Overlay

qas.q: What is the JSIG term for Level of Concerns?
qas.a: Impact Levels

qas.q: What does SCTM stand for?
qas.a: Security Controls Traceability Matrix

qas.q: What is the JSIG term that replaced the Certification Test & Evaluation, and Security Test & Evaluation Report?
qas.a: Security Assessment Report (SAR)

qas.q: What is the JSIG term for Designated Accrediting Authority?
qas.a: Authorizing Official (AO)

qas.q: Who can the Authorizing Official (AO) delegate to?
qas.a: Delegated Authorizing Official (DAO)

qas.q: JSIG term for Information Assurance Manager (IAM)?
qas.a: Information Systems Security Manager (ISSM)

qas.q: JSIG term for Information Assurance Officer (IAO)?
qas.a: Information System Security Officer (ISSO)

qas.q: JSIG term for Program Manager?
qas.a: Information System Owner (ISO)

qas.q: JSIG term for Master System Security Plan?
qas.a: Information Assurance Standard Operating Procedures (IA SOP)

qas.q: JSIG term for guest system?
qas.a: External Information System

qas.q: What is the definition of ATO?
qas.a: Approval to Operate

qas.q: What is the JSIG term for (IATO)?
qas.a: Authorization to Operate (ATO) with a Plan of Actions and Milestone (POA&M)

qas.q: Who are the RMF Decision Authorities?
qas.a:
Element Head (SAPCO)
Authorizing Official (AO)
Delegated Authorizing Official (DAO)

qas.q: Who are the RMF Assessors and Owners?
qas.a:
Security Control Assessor (SCA)
Information Owner/Steward (IO)

qas.q: Who are the RMF Implementers?
qas.a:
Information System Owner (ISO)
Information Systems Security Officer/Manager

qas.q: What is the role of the Element Head (SAPCO)?
qas.a:
• Bears ultimate responsibility for mission accomplishment and execution of business functions and all decisions made on his/her behalf

• Responsible for adequately mitigating risks to the organization, individuals, and the Nation

• Designates an Authorizing Official to make authorization decisions on behalf of Element Head (DoDM 5205.07 v1)

qas.q: What is the role of the Authorizing Official (AO)?
qas.a:
• Shall have a broad and strategic understanding of the SAP Community, his/her organization, and its place/role in the overall SAP Community

• Accountable to the Element Head for system authorization and associated risk management decision

• Authority to formally assume responsibility for operating an information system at an acceptable level of risk

qas.q: What is the role of the Delegated Authorizing Official (DAO)?
qas.a:
• Acts on behalf of the authorizing official

• Carries out and coordinates the required activities associated with security authorization (DoDM 5205.07, JSIG)

• Cannot authorize "HIGH" Impact Level systems

qas.q: What is the role of the Security Control Assessor (SCA)?
qas.a:
• Designated by the Authorizing Official

• Responsible for performing the comprehensive evaluation of the security features of an information system

• Responsible for determining the degree to which it meets its security requirements (NIST SP 800-37)

qas.q: What is the role of the Information Owner/Steward (IO)?
qas.a:
• Has statutory or operational authority for specified information and responsibility for establishing controls for its generation, classification, collection, processing, dissemination, and disposal

• Typically, in the case of Stewards of classified information, this role is also the appointed Original Classification Authority (OCA) for that particular information

qas.q: What is the role of the Information System Owner (ISO)?
qas.a:
• Responsible for overall procurement, development, integration, modification, or operation and maintenance of an IS

• Responsible for the development and maintenance of the security plan and ensures that the system is deployed and operated in accordance with the agreed-upon security controls

• Appoints the program ISSM/ISSO and ISSE

• ISSM/ISSO and ISSE may be the same person

qas.q: What is the role of the Information Systems Security Officer/Manager (ISSO/ISSM)?
qas.a:
• Responsible for the day-to-day security posture and continuous monitoring for a system

• Responsible for the overall information assurance of a program, organization, system, or enclave

qas.q: What is the Pre-RMF process?
qas.a:
Develop a Risk Assessment Report (RAR) specific to the Information System:
• Identify and prioritize risks that inform risk response decisions
• Identify asset(s)
• Identity threats
• Identify vulnerabilities & predisposing conditions
• Determine the likelihoods
• Identify Impacts
• Determine risks & uncertainties
• Communicate the results

qas.q: How many RMF steps are there?
What are the six RMF steps?
qas.a:
RMF Step 1: Categorization of the System
RMF Step 2: Selecting Security Controls
RMF Step 3: Implementing Security Controls
RMF Step 4: Assessing Security Controls
RMF Step 5: Authorizing Systems
RMF Step 6: Monitor Security Controls

qas.q: How many control families are there?
What are the 18 control families?
qas.a:
AC - Access Control
AT - Awareness and Training
AU - Audit and Accountability
CA - Security Assessment and Authorization
CM - Configuration Management
CP - Contingency Planning
IA - Identification and Authentication
IR - Incident Response
MA - Maintenance
MP - Media Protection
PE - Physical and Environmental Protection
PL - Planning
PM - Program Management
PS - Person Security
RA - Risk Assessment
SA - System and Services Acquisition
SC - System and Communications Protection
SI - System and Information Integrity

qas.q: Who is responsible for Steps 1-3 of the RMF process (Categorize, Select, Implement)?
qas.a: ISSO/ISSM

qas.q: Who is responsible for Step 4 of the RMF process (Assess)?
qas.a: Security Control Assessor (SCA)

qas.q: Who is responsible for Step 5 of the RMF process (Authorize)?
qas.a: AO/DAO

qas.q: Who is responsible for Step 6 of the RMF process (Monitor)?
qas.a: ISSO/ISSM

qas.q: What is the definition of Continuous Monitoring (CONMON)?
qas.a: Process to ensure ongoing awareness of information security, vulnerabilities, and threats to facilitate risk-based decision making.

qas.q: What are the three methods of monitoring?
qas.a:
Examine
Interview
Test

qas.q: What are the three Authorization Decisions that can be made on an RMF package?
qas.a:
Authorization to Operate
Interim Authority to Test
Denial of ATO

qas.q: What are the key artifacts for the RMF Submission Package?
qas.a:
SSP (includes the RAR, SCTM, & CONMON Plan)
SAR
POA&M

qas.q: What are the three impact ratings?
qas.a:
Three Impact Ratings
Low
Moderate
High

qas.q: What are the three impact levels?
qas.a:
Confidentiality (C)
Integrity (I)
Availability (A)

qas.q: What is the selection of the (5) Security Controls?
qas.a:
• Common
• Baseline
• Overlays
• Tailor controls
• Supplement

qas.q: What are Common Controls?
qas.a: A security control that is inherited by one or more organizational information system

qas.q: What are Compensating Security Controls?
qas.a: Management, operational, and technical controls implemented by an organization in lieu of recommended controls in the baseline list of controls

qas.q: Continuous Monitoring (ConMon) Plan
qas.a: Provides the strategy to routinely evaluate selected IA controls/metrics. Reference NIST SP 800-137, Information Security Continuous Monitoring (ISCM) for Federal Information Systems and Organizations

qas.q: Overlay
qas.a: A specification of security controls, control enhancements, supplemental guidance, and other supporting information employed during the tailoring process, that is intended to complement (and further refine) security control baselines.

qas.q: Plan of Actions and Milestones (POA&M)
qas.a: Defines plans of action and milestones related to correcting weaknesses or deficiencies, as well as reducing or eliminating known vulnerabilities and identifies completion dates.

qas.q: Risk Assessment Report (RAR)
qas.a: Defines the organizationally established level of acceptable risk associated with the operation of an IT system at a specific level; identifies risks; and provides an assessed residual-risk-level for the system

qas.q: Security Assessment Report (SAR)
qas.a: Contains security control assessment results and recommended corrective actions for security-control weaknesses and deficiencies

qas.q: System Security Plan (SSP)/Security Control Traceability Matrix (SCTM)
qas.a: Provides an overview of security requirements, description of agreed-upon controls and other supporting security-related information
